item.title:
CHINA STUDIES U.S. WAR, SEES STRATEGIC EDGE
China is analyzing Donald Trump’s war with Iran to extract military lessons, according to Western officials. Beijing is closely watching U.S. offensive capabilities and sees the strategic balance shifting in its favor in the Indo-Pacific.
Insights from the conflict could inform China’s planning for a potential future clash over Taiwan.
item.description:
CHINA STUDIES U.S. WAR, SEES STRATEGIC EDGE

China is analyzing Donald Trump’s war with Iran to extract military lessons, according to Western officials. Beijing is closely watching U.S. offensive capabilities and sees the strategic balance shifting in its favor in the Indo-Pacific.

Insights from the conflict could inform China’s planning for a potential future clash over Taiwan.

item.title:
CHINA NEARS OIL STOCKPILE DRAW
China is close to tapping commercial crude reserves as the Middle East war drags on, says FGE NexantECA. Drawdowns could reach up to 1 million barrels a day over the next 4–6 weeks to keep refineries running.
After heavy stockpiling, China holds about 1.4 billion barrels, giving it room to act—though strategic reserves are likely to remain untouched. Early signs of disruption are limited, with analysts like Antoine Halff noting any impact from Gulf supply issues may take time to reach China.
For now, Beijing is curbing fuel exports and trimming refinery runs as a precaution while trying to preserve stockpiles as long as possible.
item.description:
CHINA NEARS OIL STOCKPILE DRAW

China is close to tapping commercial crude reserves as the Middle East war drags on, says FGE NexantECA. Drawdowns could reach up to 1 million barrels a day over the next 4–6 weeks to keep refineries running.

After heavy stockpiling, China holds about 1.4 billion barrels, giving it room to act—though strategic reserves are likely to remain untouched. Early signs of disruption are limited, with analysts like Antoine Halff noting any impact from Gulf supply issues may take time to reach China.

For now, Beijing is curbing fuel exports and trimming refinery runs as a precaution while trying to preserve stockpiles as long as possible.

item.title:
STRAIT OF HORMUZ TRAFFIC DROPS SHARPLY
According to Amena Bakr, citing Kpler data, 108 vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz since the war began on 28 February—just 38 of them since 2 March.
item.description:
STRAIT OF HORMUZ TRAFFIC DROPS SHARPLY

According to Amena Bakr, citing Kpler data, 108 vessels passed through the Strait of Hormuz since the war began on 28 February—just 38 of them since 2 March.

item.description: US TO EASE VENEZUELA SANCTIONS TO UNLOCK MORE OIL AMID IRAN WAR

item.title:
RUSSIA BOOSTS IRAN’S DRONE TARGETING
Russia has supplied Iran with upgraded drone technology to help target U.S. forces in the region, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing sources familiar with the matter. Bloomberg previously reported that Moscow is also providing intelligence support, including satellite imagery and drone-targeting tactics.
item.description:
RUSSIA BOOSTS IRAN’S DRONE TARGETING

Russia has supplied Iran with upgraded drone technology to help target U.S. forces in the region, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing sources familiar with the matter. Bloomberg previously reported that Moscow is also providing intelligence support, including satellite imagery and drone-targeting tactics.

item.description: U.S. CRUDE OIL FUTURES SETTLE AT $96.21/BBL, UP $2.71, 2.90 PCT

item.title:
OIL AT $125 WOULD FORCE A GLOBAL RESET
Seth Carpenter of Morgan Stanley says oil above $125 per barrel would trigger a structural shift in the market, forcing demand destruction as supply tightens.
He outlined three possible paths:
🔸 $65–70 if prices retreat 🔸 Around $100 if the market stabilizes 🔸 $125–140 if disruptions occur in the Strait of Hormuz
Carpenter said the U.S. economy could handle $100 oil, but higher gasoline prices would strain lower-income consumers and push inflation higher. He also warned that wider supply chain disruptions could lead to actual shortages, highlighting the difference between higher prices and oil being physically unavailable.
item.description:
OIL AT $125 WOULD FORCE A GLOBAL RESET

Seth Carpenter of Morgan Stanley says oil above $125 per barrel would trigger a structural shift in the market, forcing demand destruction as supply tightens.

He outlined three possible paths:

🔸 $65–70 if prices retreat
🔸 Around $100 if the market stabilizes
🔸 $125–140 if disruptions occur in the Strait of Hormuz

Carpenter said the U.S. economy could handle $100 oil, but higher gasoline prices would strain lower-income consumers and push inflation higher. He also warned that wider supply chain disruptions could lead to actual shortages, highlighting the difference between higher prices and oil being physically unavailable.

item.description: EXPLOSION HEARD NEAR U.S. EMBASSY IN BAGHDAD, SIRENS SOUND - REUTERS WITNESS

item.title:
IRAN’S SUPREME LEADER SURVIVED STRIKE BY MINUTES
Mojtaba Khamenei narrowly survived U.S. and Israeli missile strikes that hit his residence in Tehran after stepping outside moments before the attack, according to leaked audio reported by The Telegraph.
The strikes — which killed his father Ali Khamenei and several senior Iranian officials — hit the compound on Feb. 28 with multiple missiles. Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded in the leg, while his wife and son were killed.
The recording, attributed to an official in the former leader’s office, says the attack targeted several locations in the complex and appeared aimed at eliminating the entire leadership.
item.description:
IRAN’S SUPREME LEADER SURVIVED STRIKE BY MINUTES

Mojtaba Khamenei narrowly survived U.S. and Israeli missile strikes that hit his residence in Tehran after stepping outside moments before the attack, according to leaked audio reported by The Telegraph.

The strikes — which killed his father Ali Khamenei and several senior Iranian officials — hit the compound on Feb. 28 with multiple missiles. Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded in the leg, while his wife and son were killed.

The recording, attributed to an official in the former leader’s office, says the attack targeted several locations in the complex and appeared aimed at eliminating the entire leadership.
